title: Inventory fertilizer-use, soil-nutrient, distribution-infrastructure, and fertilizer-response data sources for SSA
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates fertilizer-use survey data, AfSIS soil-nutrient data, distribution-infrastructure studies, and fertilizer-response trials into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit sub-national data granularity and soil-specific response documentation.
inputs: Africa Fertilizer Summit documentation and country data
AfSIS soil data documentation and spatial coverage
Published fertilizer distribution and pricing studies for SSA
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, geographic grain, and documented limitations — particularly sub-national data granularity and soil-response variability documentation.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering sub-national granularity, soil-nutrient coverage, and distribution-infrastructure documentation.

title: Map sub-national fertilizer access gaps for at least two SSA countries
outcome: A sub-national fertilizer-access analysis for at least two SSA countries, documenting fertilizer-use data sources, distribution-infrastructure barriers, and price-markup patterns.
inputs: National agricultural survey fertilizer-use data for target countries
Administrative boundary data for district-level aggregation
expected_artifact: validation.md update with access methodology and data-limitation documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms fertilizer-use data extraction, distribution-infrastructure documentation, and data-gap assessment for each target country.
done_condition: Fertilizer-access analysis includes methodology, sub-national results, distribution-infrastructure documentation, and replication status for at least two countries.

title: Analyze soil nutrient depletion and fertilizer response variability for target countries
outcome: A soil-nutrient depletion and fertilizer-response analysis for target countries, documenting AfSIS data coverage, nutrient-balance methodology, and soil-specific response variability with explicit research-site extrapolation limitations.
inputs: AfSIS soil nutrient data for target regions
Published fertilizer-response trials for target soil types and crops
Crop-yield and management data for nutrient-balance calculations
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with soil methodology and response-variability documentation.
validation_method: Replicator confirms soil-data methodology, nutrient-balance calculations, and response-variability documentation for each target country.
done_condition: Soil-nutrient analysis includes methodology, AfSIS coverage limitations, response-variability documentation, and replication status.

title: Red-team fertilizer-access overstatement and blanket-recommendation risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how fertilizer-access gap maps could overstate the problem by ignoring soil-specific response variability, how national averages could mask sub-national targeting needs, and how results could drive blanket fertilizer subsidies without soil-specific recommendations.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every fertilizer-access claim has sub-national data documentation, every soil claim has AfSIS coverage limitations, and every effectiveness claim has response-variability context. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every fertilizer and soil claim is either blocked, softened to data-limited language with explicit response-variability and sub-national caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match agricultural ministry and soil-health program decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the fertilizer-access and soil-nutrient analysis would change any real decision for agricultural ministries, fertilizer distributors, or soil-health programs without creating perverse incentives.
inputs: fertilizer distribution and soil-health program documentation
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for agricultural ministry and soil-health audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that national averages mask sub-national targeting needs, or that blanket fertilizer recommendations ignore soil-specific response variability. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., Kenya Ministry of Agriculture soil-fertility officer), named decision (e.g., fertilizer subsidy targeting by soil type), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for blanket fertilizer recommendations without soil-specific analysis), and replicated field-reality review.
